completed_tasks.name: 01-01 Task 1: Lock the Windows build-path invariants in a fast contract test
01-01 Task 2: Reconcile CMake and Windows batch scripts to generate the canonical dist artifacts directly
01-02 Task 1: Expand fast prereq and dist-preflight helpers without pulling in the full runtime suite
01-02 Task 2: Add a static consumer-contract test for every current dist runtime consumer

remaining_tasks.name: Wave 1 gate: clean Windows build plus root test suite
01-03 Task 1: Add explicit fast preflight npm command
01-03 Task 2: Rewrite root docs around the finalized Windows build baseline

blockers.description: Closed executor agents left a live mingw32-make build running in the main repository, which keeps writing build/wasm-build.log and contaminates any new npm run build:wasm:win gate attempts.
blockers.workaround: Before resuming, check for lingering mingw32-make/cmd/python processes from the 18:00 build window. Either wait for them to finish and inspect build/wasm-build.log, or terminate them before rerunning the Wave 1 gate.

decisions.decision: Keep 01-01 task-level verification anchored to test/wasm_build_contract.test.mjs only, with the full npm run build:wasm:win plus npm test flow reserved for the Wave 1 gate.
decisions.rationale: This keeps Nyquist feedback fast without introducing a same-wave dependency on 01-02's dist consumer test.

decisions.decision: Use node --test test/wasm_build_prereqs.test.mjs test/load_occt_factory.test.mjs as the default phase quick loop.
decisions.rationale: Those files exist at the start of Wave 1, so the validation contract stays executable before new tests are added.

decisions.decision: Do not create 01-01-SUMMARY.md or 01-02-SUMMARY.md until the Wave 1 build gate passes.
decisions.rationale: GSD treats SUMMARY.md presence as a completion marker; writing one early would incorrectly mark the plans complete while the gate is still unresolved.

next_action: Start by checking whether the lingering mingw32-make processes have exited. If they have, inspect build/wasm-build.log for success or failure and then rerun the Wave 1 gate command in a clean shell: powershell -NoProfile -Command "npm run build:wasm:win; if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { exit $LASTEXITCODE }; if (!(Test-Path 'dist/occt-js.js')) { throw 'Missing dist/occt-js.js' }; if (!(Test-Path 'dist/occt-js.wasm')) { throw 'Missing dist/occt-js.wasm' }; npm test; exit $LASTEXITCODE". Only after that passes should you create 01-01/01-02 summaries and begin 01-03.
context_notes: Wave 1 implementation landed successfully at the task level: 01-01 is in commit a5235aa and 01-02 landed as c14010d, 66c35c4, 935c446, and 4d3cfd1. Task-level tests are green. The only thing preventing Wave 2 is a contaminated build gate caused by lingering background build processes from a closed executor.
